concerns.text: Task T000a is tagged `[FR-010]` (power analysis) but its description generates a pilot dataset instead of performing the required statistical power analysis. This weakens FR-010 and drops the acceptance criterion that the power analysis must determine a sample size achieving power ≥ 0.80 for r ≥ 0.3.

Task T025a implements the Allen‑Feldman theory solver, whereas FR‑002 explicitly requires *anharmonic lattice dynamics* (ALD). Using a different methodology weakens FR‑002 and violates the specification.

Task T035 implements bootstrap resampling but does not state the required minimum of 1 000 iterations (FR‑004). Without this guarantee the bootstrap may be under‑sampled, weakening the functional requirement.
No task verifies SC‑004 (bootstrap confidence‑interval width ≤ 0.2). The specification demands a verification step; its absence means the success criterion can be missed.
No task checks that the corrected p‑value is < 0.05 (SC‑003). The analysis tasks produce p‑values but lack a pass/fail assertion, dropping the acceptance criterion.
No task enforces SC‑005 (R² statistical test must reject the null hypothesis at α = 0.05). The power‑law fit task computes R² but does not perform the required hypothesis test.

Phase 6 introduces User Story 4 (computational rule exploration) which is not present in the feature specification. Adding this story expands scope beyond the defined FRs/SCs, constituting a requirement violation.

Task T036 implements multiple‑comparison correction but does not include a verification step that the corrected p‑value meets the < 0.05 threshold (SC‑003). This omission weakens the acceptance criteria for statistical significance.
